picks.title: Ed van der Elsken. Up Close
picks.blurb: Retrospective of Netherlands' most beloved 20th-century street photographer. Nine galleries trace his evolution from 1950s reportages to final photobooks.

picks.title: LuminAir (Father's Day special)
picks.blurb: Rooftop sky bar next to Central Station; complimentary whisky cocktail for every dad this weekend.

picks.title: Martin Parr: Very Modern and Rather Ugly
picks.blurb: Tribute to British photographer's satirical, intimate anthropological look at modern life. Runs through August.

picks.title: On Rotation: Irwan, Kimberlee Ramirez, Jay Junior & guests
picks.blurb: House and funk rotating DJs at Escape's main room with weekly guest appearances.

picks.title: Queer Power
picks.blurb: World Pride celebration: queer cinema and artists from across the globe. Kicks off this weekend at Eye.
picks.why: Amsterdam Pride weekend, world-class film museum

picks.title: Sunset Yoga
picks.blurb: Free sunset yoga to end the longest day of the year with David Lloyd Clubs.

picks.title: Shufflemode
picks.blurb: Late-night music venue night at Bitterzoet with eclectic DJ programming.

picks.title: Zeehavendagen (Port Festival)
picks.blurb: Harbor open days with guided ship tours, demonstrations, free boat rides and activities for all ages.

picks.title: Sunday Clay Club — Pottery Wheel & Hand-Building
picks.blurb: Three-hour hands-on intro to clay: wheel throwing and hand-building. Shape your own ceramic piece—bowl, mug, or plate.

picks.title: Guns N' Roses & Mammoth
picks.blurb: Rock legends Guns N' Roses with opener Mammoth play Amsterdam's flagship arena venue.

picks.title: Bite of Amsterdam Food Festival
picks.blurb: Annual summer food festival kicking off season. Chefs present signature dishes and culinary experiences across Amsterdam.
picks.why: Festival weekend, launches summer dining events

picks.title: Makèz Pres. Make It Warm: Summer
picks.blurb: Summer-themed club night at intimate Basement venue with DJ collective.

picks.title: Solstice Art Festival
picks.blurb: <cite index="11-10">Open art market, exhibitions, live art performances, artists at work, and outdoor installations celebrating summer solstice</cite>.
picks.why: Free festival celebrating longest day outdoors

picks.title: No Sunday Without Techno
picks.blurb: Techno-focused Sunday night club session at John Doe with established weekly format.

picks.title: Therapeutic Flying Acro Yoga
picks.blurb: Thai massage + yoga-based acro practice to relax and unwind. All levels. Limited to 16 for personal guidance.
picks.why: Final weekend spots, all levels, hands-on movement

picks.title: Bite of Amsterdam 2026
picks.blurb: Major food festival showcasing Amsterdam's finest chefs, restaurants, and culinary talent across three days.
picks.why: Unmissable foodie event; 7 reasons to attend highlighted
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam / Bite of Amsterdam · Bite of Amsterdam official · biteofamsterdam.com

picks.title: Solstice Festival
picks.blurb: <cite index="35-3,35-4,35-5">Psychedelic festival celebrating summer solstice with Psytrance music, modern twist on tribal midsummer rituals, held at historic v
picks.why: Iconic summer solstice dance festival spanning two days
picks.source: Ruigoord / StayHappening · Amsterdam.nl Events · Activiteiten Amsterdam · Ruigoord

picks.title: Museum Market (Museumplein)
picks.blurb: <cite index="20-1,20-2,20-3">Every third Sunday market transforms Museumplein into vibrant craft hub with unique jewelry, accessories, art, live music and local
picks.why: Third Sunday craft market tradition, lively atmosphere
picks.source: AmsterdamTips · Amsterdam Tips · I amsterdam / Wanderlog

picks.title: Open Garden Days
picks.blurb: <cite index="21-3">More than 25 private and institutional gardens open doors to public, including Museum Van Loon and Huis Willet-Holthuysen</cite>.
picks.source: I amsterdam · I amsterdam, Museum van Loon · amsterdam.org

picks.title: Bacchus Winefestival
picks.blurb: Over 300 European and New World wines, curated tastings, food pairings, storytelling, and music across three weekend sessions.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Bacchus Wine Festival · Amsterdam Food Guide

picks.title: Carel Visser — sculpture in the gardens
picks.blurb: Free outdoor sculpture show — the first artist solo in the Rijksmuseum gardens, opening this weekend.

picks.title: 24 Hours Nieuw-West
picks.blurb: Free cultural event weekend in East with art installations, activities for all ages, and outdoor atmosphere. Free parking.
picks.why: Free cultural summer fest with parking
picks.source: I amsterdam Weekend Guide / Your Little  · I amsterdam, Kidsproof Amsterdam

picks.title: John Summit
picks.blurb: Electronic/house DJ brings sunset vibes to Amsterdam's outdoor lounge space on summer solstice Sunday.

picks.title: Holland Festival 2026
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's prestigious annual arts festival opens this weekend with classical and contemporary performances.

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: Yumna Al-Arashi — Body as Resistance
picks.blurb: First solo museum show for Al-Arashi — photography, sculpture and book confront the policing of women’s bodies.

picks.title: deLux: Ydro
picks.blurb: Late-night DJ set at iconic Escape, iconic Amsterdam club with state-of-the-art production.

picks.title: GRIMM (8+)
picks.blurb: Hip-hop-ballet reimagining of fairy tales—spectacular, dynamic, and darkly humorous family show.

picks.title: Spinoza — De Mokum Musical
picks.blurb: Open-air musical about philosopher Spinoza set in old Amsterdam, in the forest theatre.
picks.why: Cross-source (LBB + I amsterdam) · open-air · dry-night plan

picks.title: Escape Club
picks.blurb: Premier clubbing venue with rotating DJ sets and rotating lineup both nights of the weekend.
picks.why: Two nights of quality electronic music

picks.title: Havenfestival 2026
picks.blurb: Waterfront activities, demonstrations, and family-friendly entertainment celebrating Amsterdam's harbour.
picks.why: Free, outdoor, hands-on fun for all ages

picks.title: Bacchus Wijnfestival
picks.blurb: 300+ European wines from Amsterdam vendors; gourmet bites, live music, festival vibe. Kids free.
picks.why: Amsterdam's oldest wine festival returns for the season

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Choux
picks.blurb: Inventive, veg-forward small plates and natural wine in a raw red-brick space near the IJ.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: RIJKS®
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk’s Michelin-starred dining room beside the Rijksmuseum — the “cuisine of the Low Countries”, done with flair.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Rijsel
picks.blurb: Lille-inspired French cooking in a converted school — that rotisserie chicken is the order.

picks.title: Bar Fisk
picks.blurb: Sun-soaked Mediterranean seafood and sharing plates in De Pijp.

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: Kriterion
picks.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: Friday Next
picks.blurb: A concept store and café in one — young Dutch design to browse, with brunch and coffee while you do it.

picks.title: TonTon Club
picks.blurb: An arcade bar where you pay to play — pinball, classic cabinets and Japanese games, with beer and gyoza on tap.

picks.title: WonderWood
picks.blurb: A gallery-shop devoted to plywood and mid-century modern — bentwood chairs and design classics since 1999.

picks.title: Eye Classics
picks.blurb: Film history on the big screen: Eye's new series screens 35mm prints of canonical cinema whenever possible.

picks.title: De Filmhallen
picks.blurb: A nine-screen arthouse cinema in a converted tram depot — with a bustling food hall right next door.
picks.why: Arthouse · in the old tram depot

picks.title: ARTIS Royal Zoo
picks.blurb: The country’s oldest zoo — big cats, an aquarium and a planetarium across a leafy 19th-century park.
picks.why: All-day with kids · indoor + outdoor mix

picks.title: Eye Filmmuseum — repertory screening
picks.blurb: Across the IJ by free ferry: a striking film museum with a daily repertory programme and a river-view café.
picks.why: Indoor · a film + the ferry ride · weather-proof

picks.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
picks.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

picks.title: Zaanse Schans windmills
picks.blurb: Eight working windmills, cheese and crafts on the Zaan — free grounds, ~17 min train from Centraal.
picks.why: Short train · outdoor · classic kid day
